ddls:
CREATE TABLE "parts" (
  "part_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each part */,
  "part_number" TEXT /* Part number for identification */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the part */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the part */,
  "demand" TEXT /* Current demand forecast for the part */,
  "availability" TEXT /* Availability status of the part */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the part */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the supplier for the part */,
  "lead_time" INTEGER /* Average time taken to receive the part after ordering */,
  "min_order_quantity" INTEGER /* Minimum quantity that can be ordered */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the part for shipping calculations */,
  "dimensions" TEXT /* Dimensions of the part for storage and shipping */,
  "warranty_period" TEXT /* Warranty period for the part */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the manufacturer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("part_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parts_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES suppliers ("supplier_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parts_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent category for hierarchical categorization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "suppliers" (
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each supplier */,
  "supplier_name" TEXT /* Name of the supplier */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email contact for the supplier */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the supplier */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the supplier */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating of the supplier based on performance */,
  "payment_terms" TEXT /* Payment terms agreed with the supplier */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inventory" (
  "inventory_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each inventory record */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part in inventory */,
  "quantity_on_hand" INTEGER /* Current quantity of the part on hand */,
  "reorder_level" INTEGER /* Level at which reorder is triggered */,
  "reorder_quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity to reorder when the reorder level is reached */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the inventory */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the inventory was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("inventory_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_inventory_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the system (e.g., admin, staff) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part being ordered */,
  "quantity_ordered" INTEGER /* Quantity of the part ordered */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date when the order was placed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the order (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  "requested_by" INTEGER /* User ID who placed the order */,
  "expected_delivery_date" TEXT /* Expected date of delivery */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_requested_by FOREIGN KEY ("requested_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_details" (
  "order_detail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order detail */,
  "order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related order */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part in the order */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the part in the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_detail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_details_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_details_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "transactions" (
  "transaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transaction */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part affected by the transaction */,
  "quantity_changed" INTEGER /* Quantity changed in the inventory */,
  "transaction_date" TEXT /* Date when the transaction occurred */,
  "transaction_type" TEXT /* Type of transaction (e.g., addition, subtraction) */,
  "initiated_by" INTEGER /* User ID who initiated the transaction */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the transaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_initiated_by FOREIGN KEY ("initiated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_transactions_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part related to the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message for the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was created */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "purchase_orders" (
  "purchase_order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase order */,
  "supplier_id" INTEGER /* ID of the supplier from whom the order is made */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date when the purchase order was created */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the purchase order (e.g., pending, fulfilled) */,
  "total_amount" REAL /* Total amount of the purchase order */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Status of the payment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purchase_order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchase_orders_supplier_id FOREIGN KEY ("supplier_id") REFERENCES suppliers ("supplier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "purchase_order_details" (
  "purchase_order_detail_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase order detail */,
  "purchase_order_id" INTEGER /* ID of the related purchase order */,
  "part_id" INTEGER /* ID of the part being ordered */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the part in the purchase order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purchase_order_detail_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchase_order_details_purchase_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("purchase_order_id") REFERENCES purchase_orders ("purchase_order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchase_order_details_part_id FOREIGN KEY ("part_id") REFERENCES parts ("part_id")
)
